metadata.title: ASME B31.3-2018 Y factor library for pipe thickness web app
metadata.intended_use: Private web app lookup library for coefficient Y used in ASME B31.3 pipe pressure thickness calculation.
metadata.public_github_warning: Do not publish this file in a public GitHub Pages repository unless your organization has redistribution rights. Keep ASME-derived data private or load from a protected source.
metadata.schema_policy: Top-level metadata plus y_factor_table, material_category_aliases, lookup_policy, formula_context, and validation. Designed to be used with b31_3_2018_private_combined_stress_dataset.json.

formula_context.applies_to: Straight pipe under internal pressure for ASME B31.3 para. 304.1.2, with Y from para. 304.1.1 and Table 304.1.1. Y is also used in selected pressure design equations where explicitly referenced by the Code.
formula_context.thin_wall_condition: Use Table 304.1.1 values for t < D/6 and for materials shown in the table.
formula_context.intermediate_temperature_rule: The value of Y may be interpolated for intermediate temperatures.
formula_context.thick_wall_expression_text: For t >= D/6, para. 304.1.1 defines Y using pipe inside diameter and allowance terms. However, para. 304.1.2(b) requires special consideration for t >= D/6 or P/SE > 0.385, so the app should stop automatic calculation and require engineer review.

symbols.c: Sum of mechanical, corrosion, and erosion allowances
symbols.S: Basic allowable stress for material at design temperature
symbols.Y: Coefficient from Table 304.1.1 for t < D/6 and materials shown

boundary_labels.first: 482 degC and below, 900 degF and below
boundary_labels.last: 677 degC and above, 1250 degF and above

y_factor_table.description: Carbon steels, low-temperature carbon steels, low-alloy Cr-Mo ferritic steels, and similar ferritic steel pipe materials when allowed by ASME B31.3 material rules.
y_factor_table.above_last_temperature_policy: Use 0.7 for 677 degC and above, subject to material allowable stress limits and code applicability.
y_factor_table.interpolation_policy: Linear interpolation may be used for intermediate temperatures.

y_factor_table.description: Austenitic stainless steels such as TP304, TP304L, TP316, TP316L, TP321, TP321H, and corresponding welded pipe grades when allowed by ASME B31.3 material rules.
y_factor_table.above_last_temperature_policy: Use 0.7 for 677 degC and above, subject to material allowable stress limits and code applicability.
y_factor_table.interpolation_policy: Linear interpolation may be used for intermediate temperatures.

y_factor_table.description: Nickel alloys UNS N06617, N08800, N08810, and N08825 only. Do not apply this row to all nickel alloys without checking the code row and material specification.
y_factor_table.above_last_temperature_policy: Use 0.7 for 677 degC and above, subject to material allowable stress limits and code applicability.
y_factor_table.interpolation_policy: Linear interpolation may be used for intermediate temperatures.

y_factor_table.description: Gray iron row from Table 304.1.1. Values above 482 degC are not provided in the table and should not be extrapolated.
y_factor_table.below_first_temperature_policy: Use 0.0 for 482 degC and below where gray iron is otherwise permitted by the Code and service restrictions.
y_factor_table.above_last_temperature_policy: No value. Block automatic lookup above 482 degC.
y_factor_table.interpolation_policy: Do not interpolate across null values. Treat unavailable values as calculation stop conditions.

y_factor_table.description: Other ductile metals not covered by the specific ferritic, austenitic, or limited nickel alloy rows, when otherwise permitted by ASME B31.3 material rules.
y_factor_table.above_last_temperature_policy: Use 0.4 for 677 degC and above, subject to material allowable stress limits and code applicability.
y_factor_table.interpolation_policy: Linear interpolation may be used for intermediate temperatures, though all tabulated values are 0.4.

material_category_aliases.material_listing_warning: PSL1 is procurement/manufacturing metadata. It does not create a separate Y factor row.
Use only with a valid ASME B31.3 stress row and applicable temperature range.
Use austenitic steel Y factor. Stress row and notes remain separate from Y factor.
Use austenitic steel Y factor. Stress row and notes remain separate from Y factor.
Use austenitic steel Y factor. Stress row and notes remain separate from Y factor.
Y factor is austenitic steel, but the stress row may require TP321 variant selection by size and heat-treatment condition.
Low-temperature carbon steel. Use ferritic steel Y factor, with Table A-1M stress and notes controlling allowable stress.
Ferritic Cr-Mo alloy steel. Use ferritic steel Y factor.
Ferritic Cr-Mo alloy steel. Use ferritic steel Y factor.
Ferritic Cr-Mo alloy steel. Use ferritic steel Y factor.
CL.1 affects weld quality and Ej logic, not the Y factor row. Use austenitic steel Y factor.
CL.1 affects weld quality and Ej logic, not the Y factor row. Use austenitic steel Y factor.
CL.1 affects weld quality and Ej logic, not the Y factor row. Use austenitic steel Y factor.
CL.1 affects weld quality and Ej logic. TP321 stress row may require variant selection.
Class number affects heat-treatment/testing procurement metadata, not the Y factor row.
Class number affects heat-treatment/testing procurement metadata, not the Y factor row.

material_category_aliases.input_pattern: ASTM A672 GR.B60 CL.12 (200 BHN MAX)
material_category_aliases.material_listing_warning: Hardness limit is MTC/procurement validation metadata. It does not create a separate Y factor row.
Class number affects heat-treatment/testing procurement metadata, not the Y factor row.
Class number affects heat-treatment/testing procurement metadata, not the Y factor row.
Class number affects heat-treatment/testing procurement metadata, not the Y factor row.
Class number affects heat-treatment/testing procurement metadata, not the Y factor row.
Ferritic Cr-Mo alloy welded pipe. Class number affects procurement/testing metadata, not Y factor.
Ferritic Cr-Mo alloy welded pipe. Class number affects procurement/testing metadata, not Y factor.

Not directly listed in ASME B31.3 Table A-1M in the prepared stress dataset. Block full thickness calculation unless approved as an unlisted material with stress basis. If approved as carbon steel, ferritic steel Y factor is generally the candidate group.

Not directly listed in ASME B31.3 Table A-1M in the prepared stress dataset. Galvanizing is coating metadata, not a separate Y factor. Block full thickness calculation unless approved as an unlisted material with stress basis.

Not directly listed in ASME B31.3 Table A-1M in the prepared stress dataset. Block full thickness calculation unless approved as an unlisted material with stress basis. If approved as carbon steel, ferritic steel Y factor is generally the candidate group.

Not directly listed in ASME B31.3 Table A-1M in the prepared stress dataset. Block full thickness calculation unless approved as an unlisted material with stress basis. If approved as carbon steel, ferritic steel Y factor is generally the candidate group.
Nonmetallic thermoplastic pipe. Do not use Table 304.1.1 metallic Y factor. Use the applicable nonmetallic piping rules and material-specific design basis.

recommended_engine_flow: Normalize material description and select ASME stress row from the private stress dataset.
Read or assign y_category_id from this library.
Verify that stress row status is approved/listed before final thickness calculation.
For t < D/6, calculate Y from y_factor_table using design temperature and material category.
For intermediate temperatures, use linear interpolation between adjacent table values.
For temperature <= 482 degC, use the first tabulated value for the category.
For temperature >= 677 degC, use the last tabulated value for the category, except where the category contains null values or the material stress row has a lower maximum temperature.
For t >= D/6 or P/SE > 0.385, stop automatic calculation and require engineer review.
For unlisted materials such as IS-1239 or IS-3589, do not permit full ASME B31.3 thickness calculation unless unlisted material approval and allowable stress basis are documented.
lookup_policy.null_value_policy: A null Y value means the table does not provide a value. Do not extrapolate or silently replace with another material group.
lookup_policy.nonmetallic_policy: Do not use this metallic Y factor table for thermoplastic or other nonmetallic pipe calculations.
lookup_policy.welded_pipe_policy: Welded pipe class or weld quality affects E/Ej or procurement validation. It does not normally change the Y factor material group. Keep Y separate from weld joint quality factor logic.

logic: Find category in y_factor_table by category_id.
If designTempC <= 482, return y_values[0] if not null.
If designTempC >= 677, return y_values[last] if not null.
Find adjacent temperature bracket T1 <= designTempC <= T2.
If either adjacent Y value is null, throw error: Y factor unavailable for category and temperature.
Round for display only. Keep full numeric value for calculation.
reference_js_algorithm.display_rounding_recommendation: Show Y to 3 decimals in UI, but calculate using unrounded interpolated value.

validation.table_reference: Table 304.1.1 Values of Coefficient Y for t < D/6
validation.manual_verification_required_before_prod: Verify table values, material mapping, and code applicability against the licensed ASME B31.3-2018 copy before engineering release.
